chore(beads): concurrent issue tracker update

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1969,7 +1969,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1iqbb","title":"Local Task Safety & spawn_local Completion","description":"Goal: make spawn_local safe and fully scheduled under ThreeLane without !Send tasks ever leaving their origin worker. Resolve correctness gaps: no scheduling, illegal moves in is_local, cross-worker stealing, cancel promotion parity, and local waiter wakeups. Add tests and docs to lock the invariant.

Background: spawn_local currently stores tasks in TLS without enqueueing; LocalQueue::schedule_local exists. Local tasks can be stolen from ready lanes, violating !Send. Local cancel path does not promote already-queued tasks unlike global cancel. Completion currently wakes waiters via global queue, risking wrong-worker wakeups for local waiters.

Definition of done:
- spawn_local schedules onto a non-stealable local lane.
- !Send local tasks never cross worker boundaries.
- Cancellation promotion for local tasks matches global behavior.
- Local waiters are awakened on their owner worker.
- All tests/linters pass; regression tests added.
- Unit/regression tests explicitly assert local-task invariants (non-steal, cancel parity, waiter routing).
- E2E test script with structured logging (seed, worker id, queue sizes, local task count) proves no cross-worker leakage; run with --nocapture.","acceptance_criteria":"1. spawn_local schedules onto a non-stealable local lane; no cross-worker execution for !Send tasks.
2. Cancellation promotion for local tasks matches global behavior; local waiter routing uses owner worker.
3. Unit/regression tests assert local-task invariants (non-steal, cancel parity, waiter routing, and progress under local scheduling only).
4. E2E lab test script with structured logs (seed, worker id, queue sizes, local task count) proves no cross-worker leakage; run with --nocapture.
5. All gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (and --nocapture for new E2E).","notes":"[2026-02-11 FoggyMarsh] Epic closeout verification completed.\n\nVerified all parent-child dependents are CLOSED:\n- bd-3pnoo (Audit spawn_local enqueue + local waiter routing invariants)\n- bd-3oao9 (Local waiter routing to owner worker)\n- bd-1zaql (Local cancel promotion parity)\n- bd-1s3c0 (Non-stealable local queue + theft prevention)\n- bd-19mg7 (Local task classification + is_local borrow fix)\n- bd-iianw (spawn_local enqueue + lifecycle integration)\n- bd-na5nt (spawn_local progress test via local scheduler)\n\n closure validation rerun on shared tree:

 {"id":"bd-1jis","title":"Logical clocks: happens-before ordering for distributed events","description":"# Logical Clocks Implementation

## Goal

Implement logical clocks for ordering events in the distributed runtime, replacing wall-clock time with causally consistent ordering.

## Background

Wall clocks are unreliable in distributed systems:
- Clock skew between machines
- NTP adjustments can jump backwards
- No global 'now' across machines

Logical clocks provide:
- Happens-before ordering (causal consistency)
- Partial order on events
- Sufficient for most coordination

## Clock Types

### Lamport Clocks
Simplest logical clock:
```rust
pub struct LamportClock {
    counter: AtomicU64,
}

impl LamportClock {
    /// Tick for local event
    fn tick(&self) -> LamportTime {
        LamportTime(self.counter.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst))
    }
    
    /// Update on message receive
    fn receive(&self, sender_time: LamportTime) -> LamportTime {
        let local = self.counter.load(Ordering::SeqCst);
        let new = std::cmp::max(local, sender_time.0) + 1;
        self.counter.store(new, Ordering::SeqCst);
        LamportTime(new)
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Ord, PartialOrd, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct LamportTime(u64);
```

Properties:
- If a → b (a happens before b), then L(a) < L(b)
- But L(a) < L(b) does NOT imply a → b (concurrent events)

### Vector Clocks
Full causal ordering:
```rust
pub struct VectorClock {
    /// One counter per node
    counters: HashMap<NodeId, u64>,
    
    /// This node's ID
    self_id: NodeId,
}

impl VectorClock {
    /// Tick for local event
    fn tick(&mut self) -> VectorTime {
        *self.counters.entry(self.self_id).or_insert(0) += 1;
        VectorTime(self.counters.clone())
    }
    
    /// Update on message receive
    fn receive(&mut self, sender_time: &VectorTime) {
        for (node, &count) in &sender_time.0 {
            let entry = self.counters.entry(*node).or_insert(0);
            *entry = std::cmp::max(*entry, count);
        }
        self.tick();
    }
    
    /// Check if this happened before other
    fn happened_before(&self, other: &VectorTime) -> bool {
        // self ≤ other and self ≠ other
        self.counters.iter().all(|(k, &v)| {
            other.0.get(k).map_or(v == 0, |&ov| v <= ov)
        }) && self.counters != other.0
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct VectorTime(HashMap<NodeId, u64>);
```

Properties:
- V(a) < V(b) IFF a → b
- Neither < means concurrent

### Hybrid Logical Clocks
Best of both worlds:
```rust
pub struct HybridClock {
    /// Logical component
    logical: AtomicU64,
    
    /// Physical component (bounded wall clock)
    physical: AtomicU64,
    
    /// Maximum tolerated skew
    max_skew: Duration,
}

impl HybridClock {
    fn tick(&self) -> HybridTime {
        let physical = wall_clock_ms();
        let logical = self.logical.load(Ordering::SeqCst);
        
        let new_physical = std::cmp::max(physical, self.physical.load(Ordering::SeqCst));
        
        if new_physical == self.physical.load(Ordering::SeqCst) {
            self.logical.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
        } else {
            self.physical.store(new_physical, Ordering::SeqCst);
            self.logical.store(0, Ordering::SeqCst);
        }
        
        HybridTime { physical: new_physical, logical: self.logical.load(Ordering::SeqCst) }
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Ord, PartialOrd, Eq, PartialEq)]
pub struct HybridTime {
    physical: u64,  // Milliseconds since epoch
    logical: u64,   // Tiebreaker within same millisecond
}
```

Properties:
- Total order (unlike vector clocks)
- Bounded skew from real time
- Used by CockroachDB, YugabyteDB

## LogicalClock Trait

```rust
pub trait LogicalClock: Send + Sync {
    type Time: Clone + Ord + Send;
    
    /// Record a local event
    fn tick(&self) -> Self::Time;
    
    /// Update based on received message
    fn receive(&self, sender_time: &Self::Time) -> Self::Time;
    
    /// Get current time without ticking
    fn now(&self) -> Self::Time;
}
```

## Integration with Cx

```rust
impl Cx {
    /// Get logical timestamp
    fn logical_now(&self) -> LogicalTime;
    
    /// Tick for local event
    fn logical_tick(&self) -> LogicalTime;
    
    /// Update from message
    fn logical_receive(&self, sender_time: LogicalTime);
}
```

## Wire Protocol Integration

Every network message includes sender's logical time:
```rust
struct MessageEnvelope<M> {
    sender: NodeId,
    sender_time: LogicalTime,
    payload: M,
}
```

Receiver updates clock on every message.

## Lab Runtime

Lab runtime uses simplified logical clock:
- Deterministic counter (no wall clock)
- Same seed = same clock progression
- Virtual time mapped to logical time

## Choosing a Clock

| Clock | Size | Ordering | Use Case |
|-------|------|----------|----------|
| Lamport | O(1) | Partial | Simple coordination |
| Vector | O(nodes) | Total causal | Debug, conflict detection |
| Hybrid | O(1) | Total | Production systems |

Default: Hybrid for production, Lamport for lab testing.

## Testing

- Lamport clock increment/receive
- Vector clock happens-before
- Hybrid clock bounded skew
- Lab runtime determinism
- Wire protocol integration

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] LamportClock implementation
- [ ] VectorClock implementation
- [ ] HybridClock implementation
- [ ] LogicalClock trait
- [ ] Cx integration
- [ ] Wire protocol envelope
- [ ] Lab runtime compatibility
- [ ] Tests for all clock types","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:24:36.206463508Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:35:52.024233289Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:35:52.024153721Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clocks","distributed","phase4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jis","depends_on_id":"bd-3sst","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

## Test Categories

### 1. Rendering Tests
- Terminal capability detection
- Color support detection and downgrade
- Styled text output
- Unicode width calculation
- ANSI escape sequences

### 2. Region Tree Tests
- Simple tree rendering
- Deeply nested hierarchies
- Status icons (running, draining, etc.)
- Budget bar visualization
- Live update rendering

### 3. Diagnostic Tests
- explain_region_open accuracy
- explain_task_blocked accuracy
- Cancellation path tracing
- Obligation leak detection
- Recommendation generation

### 4. TUI Interaction Tests
- Keyboard navigation
- Region expand/collapse
- Status filtering
- Search by name/ID

### 5. Snapshot Tests
- Golden file comparisons
- Regression detection
- Output consistency

## Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual render calls
- DEBUG: Query execution, tree traversal
- INFO: Test progress, diagnostics found
- WARN: Rendering issues
- ERROR: Test failures with output diffs

## Test Scripts

### Run All Console Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync=debug,test=info"
export RUST_BACKTRACE=1

echo "=== Debug Console E2E Tests ==="
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_console -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture 2>&1 | tee console_tests.log

echo "=== Results ==="
grep -E '(PASS|FAIL|test result)' console_tests.log
```

### Update Snapshots
```bash
# When rendering changes intentionally, update golden files
BLESS=1 cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_console snapshots:: -- --nocapture
```

### TUI Interactive Test
```bash
# Manual TUI testing (requires terminal)
cargo run --example console_tui_demo
```
